source_jobs.title: Working Student – Battery Science and Data (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
About Munich Electrification

Munich Electrification is an innovative company founded with the aim of accelerating the transition to electric and connected mobility. We develop innovative electronic control units for electric vehicles and stationary storage systems for our global customers. Our international team is composed of highly motivated and exceptional engineers. As a small and specialized team we are well-aware of the importance of each individual colleague and support and promote each employee according to their abilities and needs. Our office with adjacent prototype and testing lab is located in the heart of Munich at the Heimeranplatz.

This is what we stand for:

- Technological Leadership: We challenge norms, innovate quickly, and anticipate future needs. We take smart risks and learn from outcomes to stay ahead.

- Sustainability: We actively drive change for a sustainable future, aware of our impact and inspiring others to build a better world.

- Team Culture: We believe work should be enjoyable, fostering a spirit of inspiration, celebration, and teamwork across the globe.

- Entrepreneurial Spirit: We embrace change, think big, and push limits with passion.



Your role

We are looking for a working student to join our Battery Science and Data team. You will work directly with our engineers to gain hands-on experience in physics-based modeling, data analysis, and cloud- based simulation. This is your chance to deepen your knowledge of electric vehicle batteries and the simulation frameworks used to model their real-world behavior.

We are hiring several working students for different areas in our team. You will focus on one of them, so you do not need to bring the full list below. Just show us where your strengths are.



Your future tasks at a glance

 - Support the development and validation of battery cell models and high-level algorithms (e.g., SOx estimation) by running simulations and analyzing results.

 - Help build and maintain our cloud data platform by structuring raw IoT logs, designing data models, and improving time-series storage as well as query performance.

 - Support the team with deployments in test cars and monitor OTA (Over-the-Air) updates to ensure data integrity.

 - Evaluate, implement, and document proof of concepts for future algorithms or new data analysis tools.



What you bring with you

 - Student in electrical engineering, physics, mathematics, computer science, software engineering, or a similar field.

 - Confident working in a high-level programming language (Python preferred).

 - Basic understanding of general electronics, embedded systems, and IoT devices.

 - Willingness to take responsibility for a project and not to be discouraged by setbacks.

 - Preference for working in teams and strong communication skills (English and German preferred).

 - Ideally, you also have Experience with at least one of: data analysis in Python, databases (SQL / time-series) or cloud/infrastructure tooling (AWS, Docker, Git).

 - Knowledge of dynamical systems (ODEs) or state estimation methods (e.g., Kalman filters).

 - Initial insights into modeling lithium-ion battery behavior.

 - Exposure to machine learning frameworks.



Our Offer & Benefits

The chance to be part of a highly innovative, agile, and unique team with prestigious customers in the automotive and battery storage sector. You will gain a deeper knowledge around connected & electric vehicle batteries, battery management and electronics development and strive in an exciting work environment

Benefits (Full-time Employees):

- Trust-based working hours and hybrid work

- Adequate and competitive compensation

- Pension Plan/Bonus

- Free access to the fitness center right next to us or subsidized EGYM Wellpass

- Free snacks, coffee, drinks and lunch (freshly cooked by our chef) every day

- Public transport ticket

- Bike-Leasing via Business Bike

- Experience various inspiring and fun team events

- ME-branded clothing

You will drive the full development cycle—from initial concept to high-volume production—leveraging your expertise in HV-component design and automotive durability standards. You don't just design parts; you build the structural backbone that protects our electronics in the harshest automotive environments.



Your future tasks at a glance

 - End-to-End Ownership: Responsibility for (electro-)mechanical products and components from initial ideation and 3D CAD design (PTC Creo) to final production release.

 - Standards-Driven Design: Design components according to ISO 16750 (environmental loads) and ensure compliance with LV 124 / LV 148 for mechanical and climatic durability.

 - High-Voltage Safety: Implement mechanical safeguards for HV components, focusing on creepage and clearance distances and structural isolation to meet ISO 26262 and SAE J2929 safety goals.

 - Thermal Architecture: Lead the mechanical integration of thermal interface materials (TIMs) and heat-sinking solutions to manage heat in high-current balancing circuits.

 - Design Verification Leadership: Define and execute/coordinate engineering tests together with the validation engineers in our lab, correlating physical results with simulation to ensure robustness against Shock & Vibration and Thermal cycling.

 - Supplier & Lifecycle Management: Act as the technical lead for supplier technical reviews and manage the Bill of Materials (BOM) within PLM systems.



What you bring with you

 - Degree in Mechanical or Mechatronics Engineering.

 - 5+ years in automotive mechanical design, with a deep understanding of LV and AEC-Q qualified component housing.

 - Expertise in high-volume processes like injection molding and precision stamping for battery interconnects.

 - Advanced proficiency in PTC Creo and experience with PLM/PDM workflows (Windchill/Teamcenter).

 - Familiarity with UN 38.3 (battery transport safety) and general automotive environmental testing.

The chance to be part of a highly innovative, agile, and unique team with prestigious customers in the automotive and battery storage sector. - Entrepreneurial Spirit: We embrace change, think big, and push limits with passion.



Your Role

As a Supply Chain Analyst, you will be responsible for establishing and developing partnerships with our electronic component suppliers to ensure their production capacity for our product demands. Furthermore, you will be responsible for the supply chain forecasting and together with our Operations Managers, you will coordinate and manage the ME multi-source strategy. As a Supply Chain Analyst, you will establish a Supply Chain Monitoring system to track and analyze our product development life cycle and the supply chain status with the aim to identify potential obsolete components and/or supply bottlenecks. You will be in regular contact with our Production Partners and key component manufacturers and you will be the main contact for all topics related to our component supply.



Your Responsibilities

 - Establish a solid and continuous communication with our component key manufacturers and our EMS partners.

 - Develop the ME Multi Source Strategy to ensure the continuous supply from all relative suppliers.

 - Evaluate and track the lifecycle status of each supplied electronic component for our products.

 - Supporting our Operations Team to establish the right procurement strategy and forecast with our production partners.

 - EMS stock evolution tracking and mid/long term purchase strategy development.

 - Define and prepare supply chain reports for our Management.

 - Development and refinement of processes and documentation.



Your Profile

 - Multiple years of relevant work experience in the electronic business.

 - A good understanding of electronics and their manufacturing methods.

 - Excellent stakeholder and supplier management skills, equally focused on product quality and on-time delivery.

 - A structured, independent way of working and good negotiation skills.

 - Highly motivated, willing to take responsibility and ownership of a project.
